name: Flushing Bank
description_short: Community-based bank operating in NY offering personal and business checking, savings, lending, and mobile banking services with local branch presence.
description_long:
Flushing Bank is a New York-based community bank that positions itself with the tagline 'Small enough to know you. Large enough to help you.' The bank operates multiple branch locations across Queens, Brooklyn, Manhattan, and Long Island, providing customers with both in-person and digital banking options. As of October 2020, Flushing Bank merged with OceanFirst, expanding its service capabilities and market reach.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of banking and lending products designed for both personal and business customers. Personal banking services include checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), mobile banking, bill pay, check deposit via mobile app, and account balance viewing. Lending solutions encompass business loans, commercial mortgages, and residential mortgages with competitive rates and flexible terms. Business banking customers can access dedicated checking and savings accounts, cash management solutions, and merchant services. The bank also provides money transfer capabilities through Zelle and maintains ATM access through both Flushing Bank-owned ATMs and AllPoint network locations.

Flushing Bank distinguishes itself through its community-focused relationship banking model, combining the product depth and convenience of larger commercial banks with personalized service typical of local institutions. The bank actively educates customers about security threats, including detailed information about phone spoofing scams, and emphasizes that it will never contact customers requesting login credentials, authentication codes, or PIN numbers. They offer a mobile banking app with comprehensive functionality and maintain a branch locator tool for customer convenience.

The bank operates as a traditional full-service community bank without the predatory lending practices associated with payday lenders or check cashers. However, specific product details like APR ranges, minimum deposit requirements, loan terms, and fee schedules are not disclosed on the website, requiring customers to contact the bank directly for detailed pricing information. The recent merger with OceanFirst suggests ongoing corporate restructuring that may impact service consistency during transition periods. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal checking accounts with cash bonus rewards
Personal savings accounts and Certificates of Deposit (CDs)
Cash management solutions for business customers
Business loans and lines of credit
Mobile banking app with check deposit, bill pay, and account management
ATM network access (Flushing Bank and AllPoint)
pros: Multi-location branch network across Queens, Brooklyn, Manhattan, and Long Island with walk-in and appointment availability
Mobile banking app enabling check deposits, bill pay, balance viewing, and account management from smartphone
Diverse lending solutions including business loans, commercial mortgages, and residential mortgages with competitive rates
Cash management and merchant services for business customers
Zelle money transfer integration for fast, safe fund transfers
Dedicated security education including phone spoofing awareness and protection tips
Community-based relationship banking approach combining personalized service with institutional resources
cons: Website does not disclose APR ranges, interest rates, minimum deposits, or fee structures for any products
Limited geographic footprint restricted to New York area only
Ongoing merger integration with OceanFirst (as of October 2020) may create service disruptions or policy changes
No information provided about CD term lengths, early withdrawal penalties, or promotional rates
Business loan and mortgage application processes, approval timelines, and qualification requirements not detailed online
best_for: New York-based consumers seeking community banking with personalized service and modern digital convenience
Small business owners in NY needing business checking, merchant services, and cash management solutions
Homebuyers and property investors in the NY area seeking residential and commercial mortgage financing
Customers prioritizing local branch access and relationship-based banking over online-only institutions
diagnosis: Flushing Bank is best for New York-area customers seeking traditional community banking with modern digital conveniences and personalized service. The main caveat is that detailed pricing information (APRs, fees, minimum deposits) is not available online, requiring direct contact with the bank for specific product terms and qualification requirements.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
